modes.name: Tercile Uniformity
modes.description: Residual-tercile labels collapse toward uniform or bland class assignments, making noisy controls look serious.
diagnostic_tests: distance from noisy control to serious solvers
modes.example_scenario: noisy_solver nearest neighbor is svr_rbf under aggregate certificate metrics.
modes.paper_use: Explains why R/S/N should not be treated as ordinary supervised classes.

modes.name: Label-Solver Coupling
modes.description: The label construction encodes the behavior of a particular solver rather than solver-independent representation evidence.
modes.example_scenario: R/S/N assignments change primarily when the solver changes, not when representation evidence changes.
modes.paper_use: Motivates label-free Y-U over supervised label heads.

modes.name: Target-Difficulty Conflation
modes.description: Hard targets receive worse labels because the target is difficult, not because the representation is noisy.
modes.example_scenario: high-N-ceiling tasks are marked noisy even when all solvers agree the target is intrinsically hard.
modes.paper_use: Separates task recoverability from representation quality.

modes.name: Scalar Projection
modes.description: A single certificate projection such as alpha misranks solver quality by hiding other axes such as sigma.
diagnostic_tests: alpha rank vs actual performance rank
modes.example_scenario: mean_baseline has highest alpha but high sigma, so alpha-alone ranking is misleading.
modes.paper_use: Direct support for 'compatibility is not scalar'.

modes.name: Gate Compression
modes.description: A multi-axis certificate is reduced to one final gate decision, erasing meaningful metric separation.
diagnostic_tests: metric range conditional on same gate
modes.example_scenario: all S018D solvers EXECUTE despite visible alpha/N/sigma differences.
modes.paper_use: Shows typed decisions can become scalar-like when thresholds erase structure.

modes.name: Range Compression
modes.description: Serious solvers occupy a narrow certificate band, limiting fine-grained discrimination.
modes.example_scenario: pca_v1, spatial_lag_v1, and gnn_v2 cluster tighter than the broad serious-solver panel.
modes.paper_use: Explains why coarse separation can coexist with failed fine routing.

modes.name: Target-Solver Conflation
modes.description: The evaluation confuses hard-for-this-target with incompatible-for-this-solver.
modes.example_scenario: mean pairwise alpha-profile correlation is low, so what is hard for one solver is not hard for another.
modes.paper_use: Defines why target averaging is dangerous.

modes.name: Proxy Calibration Drift
modes.description: Proxy kappa or proxy certificate metrics decouple from true solver success or expected control behavior.
modes.example_scenario: kappa is compressed and noisy_solver is not exposed as noisy by aggregate metrics.
modes.paper_use: Turns proxy limitations into an explicit evaluation target.

modes.name: Fine-Routing Failure
modes.description: Certificate evidence supports coarse diagnosis but cannot select among serious solvers or route per sample.
modes.example_scenario: certificate separates controls but not serious solvers well enough for routing.
modes.paper_use: Prevents overclaiming that GeoCert solves routing.
